title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is a mix of practical governance and system-behavior work: new proposals for auditable compliance monitoring, fresh evidence on moderation effects, and product-level safety updates aimed at better handling sensitive conversations. The research slate also leans toward auditability, privacy, and access control rather than headline model capability gains.

items.title: Helping ChatGPT better recognize context in sensitive conversations
items.why_it_matters: Improved context tracking in sensitive chats can change real-world safety performance, escalation, and harm detection.

items.title: Moving from Theory to Action in AI Risk Management
items.why_it_matters: It pushes AI risk management from principles toward operational practices organizations can actually implement.

items.title: Formal Methods Meet LLMs: Auditing, Monitoring, and Intervention for Compliance of Advanced AI Systems
items.why_it_matters: It proposes concrete methods for lifecycle auditing and compliance monitoring of advanced AI systems.

items.title: Position: AI as Part of Self -- Extending the Mind Requires Cognitive Co-Regulation
items.why_it_matters: It reframes alignment around human-AI co-regulation, with implications for interface and safeguard design.

items.title: Who, Why, and How: Disentangling the Effects of Moderation Source, Context, and Language on Post-Removal Behavior
items.why_it_matters: It studies how moderation design affects user behavior, informing safer and more effective enforcement policies.

items.title: Fully Open Meditron: An Auditable Pipeline for Clinical LLMs
items.why_it_matters: Auditability and provenance are especially important for clinical LLM deployment and external validation.

items.title: The Privacy Price of Tail-Risk Learning: Effective Tail Sample Size in Differentially Private CVaR Optimization
items.why_it_matters: It clarifies privacy-utility tradeoffs when optimizing rare but high-impact risks under differential privacy.

items.title: LymphNode: A Plug-and-Play Access Control Method for Deep Neural Networks
items.why_it_matters: Model access controls could reduce extraction and inversion risks in edge and third-party deployments.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
